# `expand` Function

Expand a flat path-keyed map into a nested map

Splits each key on the separator and rebuilds the nested object structure. Inverse of collapse.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
expand(input dynamic, separator string...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The flat map or object with path-based keys to expand.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `separator` (Variadic, String, Nullable) Separator used to split key segments. Defaults to '/'.

## Example Usage

### Basic usage

`expand` takes a flat map with path-based keys and reconstructs the nested structure. It is the inverse of `collapse`.

```terraform
locals {
  flat = {
    "dev/api/port"   = 8080
    "dev/web/port"   = 443
    "prod/api/port"  = 8080
    "prod/web/port"  = 443
  }
}

output "expanded" {
  value = provider::util::expand(local.flat)
}
# Result:
# {
#   dev  = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = { port = 443 } }
#   prod = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = { port = 443 } }
# }
```

### Custom separator

Pass a string to specify the separator used in the flat keys (default comes from the provider configuration).

```terraform
output "from_dot_keys" {
  value = provider::util::expand({
    "a.b.c" = "deep value"
    "a.b.d" = "another value"
    "x.y"   = "shallow"
  }, ".")
}
# Result: { a = { b = { c = "deep value", d = "another value" } }, x = { y = "shallow" } }
```
